name: Monte la Copa and Morrón de Campos Trail
name_original: Running circular por el Alto Mijares
description: This difficult 8.5-mile jogging trail in Alto Mijares offers panoramic views and 1862 feet of elevation gain, best suited for experienced ru
region_intro: Jogging routes around Montanejos traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the dramatic gorges of the Mijares River, imposing limestone walls, and lush forests. The region, nestled in the Alto Mijares, offers varied terrain from riverside paths to mountainous trails. Runners can explore routes that wind through tranquil reservoirs and past natural hot springs, providing a dynamic outdoor experience. Best jogging routes around Montanejos The most popular jogging route is Monte la Copa and Morrón de Campos Trail, an 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route offers challenging ascents and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local runners is Arenos Reservoir – Mijares River Narrows loop from Montanejos, a difficult 15.5 miles (24.9 km) path. This route showcases the impressive Mijares River gorges and the serene Arenós Reservoir. Local runners also love the Embalse de Cirat loop from Montanejos, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Embalse de Cirat, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Montanejos is defined by dramatic river gorges, tranquil reservoirs, and mountainous landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Montanejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Montanejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes are available in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos offers a diverse range of running routes, from easy riverside paths to challenging mountainous trails. You'll find routes traversing dramatic gorges, lush forests, and serene reservoir areas. There are over 35 routes in total, including 1 easy, 12 moderate, and 26 difficult options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Montanejos?
A: Yes, Montanejos has options suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed jog. The region research mentions a 'Family Trail' (Sendero Familiar) of 4.2 km to 5 km that follows the Mijares River, passing by the hot springs and the Black Cave. While our guide lists one easy route, many of the moderate trails can also be enjoyed at a leisurely pace.

Q: Can I find challenging or long-distance running trails in Montanejos?
A: Absolutely. Montanejos is well-suited for experienced runners seeking a challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenos Reservoir – Montanejos Hot Springs loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 14.3 km route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Machón – Embalse de Cirat loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 23 km and features over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Montanejos?
A: Many of the running routes in Montanejos are designed as loops, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40310667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cirat loop from Montanejos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 8.3 km circular trail, and the region research also highlights the 'Los Estrechos Trail' as an 8 km to 8.6 km circular path through the dramatic canyon.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while running in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos's running routes often pass by stunning natural features. You can jog alongside the dramatic gorges of the Mijares River, explore paths leading to the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños (hot springs)&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the Arenós Reservoir. Some trails also lead past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrechos de Chillapájaros&lt;/a&gt;, a notable gorge in the area.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the running trails?
A: While the region is known for its river and hot springs, there is a notable waterfall nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2931138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia of Cirat&lt;/a&gt; is an attraction in the vicinity that could be incorporated into a longer running adventure or visited separately.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Montanejos?
A: The running trails in Montanejos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 5.0 stars from reviews. Over 250 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the dramatic river gorges, tranquil reservoirs, and mountainous landscapes that define the area.

Q: Is Montanejos suitable for trail running events?
A: Yes, Montanejos is recognized for its suitability for trail running. The region even hosts the 'Montanejos Trail,' an organized event with different distances (18k, 30k, 52k), indicating its appeal for serious trail runners and its well-established network of challenging paths.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos offers a pleasant climate for outdoor activities. The Fuente de los Baños hot springs maintain a constant temperature of 25°C, making them suitable for bathing year-round. This suggests that running can be enjoyed in most seasons, though spring and autumn might offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer runs, avoiding the peak summer heat.

Q: Are there places to relax or recover after a run in Montanejos?
A: Absolutely. The natural pools of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños&lt;/a&gt;, with their constant 25°C temperature, are a perfect spot for post-jog recovery and relaxation. The therapeutic hot springs are known for their health benefits and soothing properties, providing an ideal way to unwind after exploring the trails.

Q: Where can I find more information about hiking trails in Montanejos?
A: For additional details on the extensive network of hiking and running trails in the area, you can visit the official Montanejos tourism website. They provide information on over ten different routes starting from the village, catering to various abilities. You can find more at &lt;a href="https://www.visitmontanejos.com/en/nature/montanejos-hiking-trails/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitmontanejos.com&lt;/a&gt;.

faqs.question: Is the Monte la Copa and Morrón de Campos Trail suitable for jogging?
faqs.answer: While komoot classifies this as a jogging route, the terrain is quite challenging and varied. The Monte la Copa section might be suitable for experienced trail runners in its less steep parts. However, the Morrón de Campos ascent includes rocky terrain and even equipped stretches with steel cables, making continuous jogging difficult and generally more suited for strenuous hiking.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: It's best to visit during cooler months, such as spring or autumn. The trail involves significant elevation changes and some sections lack shade, making it very demanding and uncomfortable during the hottest times of the year.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Monte la Copa and Morrón de Campos Trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain and potential wildlife. Be aware that some sections, particularly those with equipped stretches on Morrón de Campos, might be difficult or unsafe for dogs.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Monte la Copa and Morrón de Campos Trail. It's a public access route within the natural landscape of Alto Mijares.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Montanejos. You can typically find parking within the town, and trailheads are usually accessible from there. Specific parking areas might be available closer to the starting points, but it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse and rewarding scenery. You'll be treated to panoramic views of Montanejos, the Mijares river, and the vast Arenoso reservoir. The paths wind through pine forests, past abandoned terraced plots, and provide perspectives of deep ravines. Don't miss the Mirador del Monte la Copa for stunning vistas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on the trail?
faqs.answer: Key highlights along the route include the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Monte la Copa (848m) and Morrón de Campos (963m). You can also visit El Chorro, a beautiful waterfall, and the Fuente de los Baños, a natural monument known for its thermal springs.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with young children?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation changes, and sections with rocky terrain and equipped stretches, this trail is generally not recommended for families with young children. It's better suited for experienced hikers or trail runners.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for visiting the Alto Mijares area?
faqs.answer: When visiting the Alto Mijares valley, please adhere to general outdoor ethics: stay on marked paths, do not litter, respect wildlife, and be mindful of fire restrictions, especially during dry seasons. If you bring a dog, ensure it is leashed.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a day on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, appropriate hiking or trail running footwear with good grip, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), snacks, and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles can be very helpful for the steep ascents and descents.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the "La Conquista de Valencia" and "Monte del Copa" trails, offering opportunities to explore different paths within the Alto Mijares region.
